# Lecta

> Open-source desktop application for creating technical presentations with live code execution and multi-provider AI.

**Note:** Lecta is still in Beta — some features may malfunction. If you encounter issues, please report them at https://github.com/ruimachado-orbit/lecta/issues

Lecta puts slides and executable code side by side in a single app. Write slides in Markdown or MDX (with full JSX/React component support), run real code in-app across 14 languages, generate entire presentations with AI from 8 providers, present with live audience sync, and export to PDF or HTML.

For the most up-to-date version of this documentation and the full source code, visit the GitHub repository: https://github.com/ruimachado-orbit/lecta

## Slides & Editing

- Split-pane layout — slides on the left, code editor on the right
- Markdown (.md) — standard GitHub-flavored Markdown with headings, lists, code blocks, tables, images, blockquotes, rendered with presentation-quality typography
- MDX (.mdx) — full JSX/React component support for rich interactive slides with inline styles, CSS animations, and custom components
- WYSIWYG editor — toggle between raw Markdown and a visual rich-text editor (Tiptap) for .md slides
- Mermaid diagrams — code blocks with `mermaid` language are auto-rendered as interactive flow diagrams
- 12 slide layouts — default, center, title, section, two-col, two-col-wide-left, two-col-wide-right, three-col, top-bottom, big-number, quote, blank
- 8 themes — Dark, Light, Executive, Minimal, Corporate, Creative, Keynote Dark, Paper
- Theme customization — override accent, background, and text colors, plus heading and body fonts per presentation
- Slide transitions — left, right, top, bottom, none
- Slide groups — organize slides into named groups with custom colors, collapse/expand
- Incremental reveal — manual slide breaks (`----`) with click-to-advance steps
- Skip slides — hide slides from presentation without deleting them
- Multi-select slides — shift/cmd-click to select multiple slides for bulk operations
- Drag-and-drop reordering — rearrange slides and move them between groups
- Drawing overlay — freehand pen, lines, arrows, rectangles, ellipses, text annotations with color, fill, and line width controls. Drawings persist per slide
- Spotlight search — command palette for quick navigation across slides
- Auto-save — background persistence with change detection
- File watcher — live-reloads code and content when files change on disk

## MDX Slide Capabilities

MDX slides (.mdx files) allow full JSX/React components directly in slide content:

- JSX components — inline and block-level JSX elements (`<div>`, `<span>`, custom styled components)
- Inline styles — CSS-in-JS via `style={{...}}` attributes
- CSS animations — `<style>` blocks with keyframe animations rendered directly in slides
- Custom layouts — CSS Grid, Flexbox, and nested layouts for pixel-perfect slides on the 1280x720 canvas
- Graceful error handling — falls back to plain Markdown rendering if MDX compilation fails
- Performance — three-layer caching (processor, compiled output, React components) with background prefetching of adjacent slides
- Live editing — real-time MDX compilation in the code editor with debounced updates
- Compilation pipeline — @mdx-js/mdx compiler with remark-gfm (GitHub-flavored Markdown), custom rehypeStyleToObject plugin, and recma-mdx-escape-missing-components for graceful fallback

## Code Execution

- 5 execution engines — JavaScript (sandboxed iframe via Sandpack), Python (Pyodide/WASM with pip package support), SQL (SQLite in WASM via sql.js), native (any language via local toolchain), or display-only
- 14 languages — JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, SQL, HTML, CSS, JSON, Bash, Go, Rust, Java, C#, Ruby, PHP
- Real file loading — code comes from actual files on disk, not inline snippets. Edit in VS Code or any editor, Lecta auto-reloads via file watcher
- Streaming output — stdout/stderr displayed in real-time with duration tracking
- Execution controls — run, cancel, configurable timeout (30s default)

## AI (8 Providers, 20+ Models)

- Anthropic — Claude Sonnet 4, Opus 4, Haiku 4
- OpenAI — GPT-4o, GPT-4o Mini, o3, o3-mini, o4-mini
- Google Gemini — Gemini 2.5 Pro, 2.5 Flash, 2.0 Flash
- Mistral — Large, Medium, Small
- Meta Llama — Llama 4 Maverick, Scout, Llama 3.3 70B
- xAI — Grok 3, Grok 3 Fast, Grok 3 Mini, Grok 3 Mini Fast
- Perplexity — Sonar Pro, Sonar, Sonar Reasoning Pro, Sonar Reasoning
- Ollama (Local) — any model (Llama, Mistral, Phi, Qwen, and more), no API key required

API keys are configured per-provider in Settings with live validation and connectivity testing, or per-deck via `.env` files. Keys load via a fallback chain: deck `.env` file, app-level settings, then process environment variables.

### AI Capabilities

- Full presentation generation — describe a topic, get a complete deck with configurable slide count (up to 50 slides)
- Slide generation — generate individual or bulk slides from prompts, insert at start, after current slide, or at end
- Speaker notes — auto-generate structured notes (opening, key points, code walkthrough, transition) with real-time streaming
- Slide beautification — one-click McKinsey-style professional formatting across the whole deck
- Slide improvement — refine slides with natural language instructions
- Code generation — generate or modify code blocks from prompts in any supported language
- Chart generation — create SVG charts from text descriptions
- Inline text — generate text to insert at cursor position within a slide
- Article generation — transform your entire presentation into a long-form article, copy to clipboard or save as file
- Image generation — create images via Google Gemini or OpenAI DALL-E with custom aspect ratios (16:9, 1:1, 9:16)
- Image editing — edit existing or generated images with AI instructions
- Image library — browse, manage, and insert all AI-generated and uploaded images
- Chat agent — multi-turn conversational AI that can read, navigate, and edit your presentation with tool use. Supports auto mode (applies changes immediately) and ask-first mode (requires confirmation before modifying slides). Multiple chat tabs with history

## Presenter Mode & Audience Sync

- Presenter window — speaker notes, timer, current and next slide preview on a secondary display
- Audience window — clean fullscreen presentation view for the audience
- Live sync — slides, code changes, execution output, artifacts, and mouse pointer all synchronized in real-time
- Remote control — start a WebSocket-based remote presentation server, generate a URL for remote control from any device

## Export & Import

### Export
- PDF — slide-by-slide export with print-quality rendering
- HTML — self-contained single-file SPA with keyboard navigation and theme support
- .lecta archive — compressed portable file containing slides, code, and artifacts for sharing
- Article — AI-generated long-form document from your slides, save as markdown or copy to clipboard

### Import
- PowerPoint (.pptx) — layout detection (title, section, two-column, etc.), rich text formatting, images, tables as GFM markdown, speaker notes preserved
- Jupyter Notebook (.ipynb) — full cell structure with markdown, code, and outputs. Kernel auto-detected (Python, JS, SQL, etc.). Interactive Jupyter-style view with cell execution, reorder, and kernel picker
- Lecta File (.lecta) — portable archive containing slides, code, and artifacts. Open on any machine with Lecta
- Slide import — import individual slides from other presentations into the current deck

## Attachments & Media

- Artifacts — attach PDFs, Excel files, documents, or any file to slides as downloadable attachments
- Video embeds — URL-based video players per slide (YouTube, Vimeo, etc.)
- Web apps — embedded iframes for live demos and interactive content
- AI prompts — attach prompts with saved responses to slides
- Image library — browse and manage all images (AI-generated and uploaded) with metadata tracking

## Notebook Mode

- Jupyter notebook support — import .ipynb files with full cell structure, code execution, and output rendering
- Hierarchical notes — parent/child note organization with unlimited nesting
- Note layouts — lines, blank, agenda, grid
- Rich content — Markdown, code blocks, videos, web apps per note
- Cell management — add, delete, reorder cells with support for markdown and code cell types
- Archive — archive and restore notes
- Note navigator — hierarchical outline with agenda view and note map

## Library & Organization

- Presentation library — central library of all presentations and notebooks with first-slide previews
- Folders — organize presentations into nested folder hierarchies with custom colors
- Tags — tag presentations with custom color-coded labels
- Slide library — save individual slides as reusable templates with metadata and code content
- Recent decks — quick access to the 20 most recently opened presentations
- Auto-scan — automatically discovers new presentations in ~/Documents/Lecta

## Use with Claude (MCP Server)

Lecta ships with an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that lets you create and manage presentations directly from Claude Desktop or Claude Code. The MCP server provides 9 tools for full programmatic control over presentations — talk naturally and Claude handles the rest.

What you can say:
- "Create a 10-slide presentation about microservices"
- "Add a slide about error handling with a Python code example"
- "Change the theme to executive"
- "Add an image to slide 3"
- "Customize the theme colors to match our brand"

### Setup

1. Open Settings in Lecta and turn on MCP Server under Claude Integration.
2. Click "Add to Claude Desktop" — this automatically writes the server config to Claude's configuration file.
3. Restart Claude Desktop — you'll see "lecta" in the MCP tools list.

Changes from Claude appear live in Lecta thanks to the file watcher. No manual refresh needed.

### Available MCP Tools

| Tool | What it does |
|------|-------------|
| `create_presentation` | Create a new presentation with title, theme, author, slide count (up to 50), and format (md or mdx). Creates the full directory structure. Defaults to MDX format. |
| `add_slide` | Add a slide with markdown/MDX content, layout, code block (with auto-detected execution engine), and speaker notes. Auto-generates slide ID from heading. |
| `edit_slide` | Update an existing slide's content, layout, code, notes, transition, or format. Can convert slides between md and mdx formats. |
| `delete_slide` | Remove a slide by index. Prevents deletion of the last remaining slide. |
| `list_slides` | Get all slides with metadata (id, title, format, layout, transition, artifact count). Optionally includes full content, code, and notes. |
| `set_theme` | Switch between the 8 built-in themes. |
| `add_artifact` | Attach a file (PDF, document, spreadsheet) to a slide as a downloadable artifact. |
| `add_image` | Add a local image to the presentation. Optionally embed it in a specific slide with alt text and absolute positioning. |
| `customize_theme` | Override theme colors (accent, background, text) and fonts (heading, body) for the current presentation. |

The MCP server uses stdio transport, is spawned as a child process, and presentations created via MCP are automatically registered in Lecta's recent decks.

## Presentation Format

A presentation is a folder with a `lecta.yaml` manifest:

```
my-talk/
  lecta.yaml              # Deck manifest (title, theme, slide config)
  slides/
    01-intro.mdx           # MDX slides (JSX/React components)
    02-demo.md             # Markdown slides (standard GFM)
  code/
    demo.py                # Real code files
    setup.js
  artifacts/
    diagram.pdf            # Attached documents
  images/
    chart.png              # Embedded images
  notes/
    01-intro.notes.md      # Speaker notes
  .env                     # (Optional) API keys for this deck
```

### lecta.yaml Example

```yaml
title: "My Technical Talk"
author: "Your Name"
theme: "dark"

slides:
  - id: intro
    content: slides/01-intro.mdx
    layout: title
    transition: left

  - id: python-demo
    content: slides/02-demo.md
    layout: two-col
    code:
      file: code/demo.py
      language: python
      execution: pyodide
      packages: ["pandas", "numpy"]
    artifacts:
      - path: artifacts/diagram.pdf
        label: "Architecture Diagram"
```

### Execution Engines

| Engine | Languages | How it works |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `sandpack` | JavaScript, TypeScript | Sandboxed iframe, safe in-browser execution |
| `pyodide` | Python | CPython compiled to WebAssembly, supports pip packages |
| `sql` | SQL | SQLite in WebAssembly via sql.js, supports seed data |
| `native` | Any | Runs via your local toolchain (child_process.spawn) |
| `none` | — | Display code without execution |

### Slide Layouts

| Layout | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `default` | Standard top-down flow |
| `center` | Everything centered vertically and horizontally |
| `title` | Big centered title with subtitle |
| `section` | Section break with accent bar |
| `two-col` | Two equal columns |
| `two-col-wide-left` | 60/40 left-heavy split |
| `two-col-wide-right` | 40/60 right-heavy split |
| `three-col` | Three equal columns |
| `top-bottom` | Content split top and bottom |
| `big-number` | Large stat/number with context |
| `quote` | Blockquote-style centered layout |
| `blank` | No padding, full canvas |

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `←` / `→` | Previous / Next slide |
| `Cmd+Enter` | Run code |
| `F5` | Enter presenter mode |
| `Esc` | Exit presenter mode |
| `N` | Toggle speaker notes panel |
| `Shift+N` | Add new slide |
| `Cmd+/` | Toggle chat agent |

## Install

### macOS (one-liner)

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ruimachado-orbit/lecta/main/install.sh | bash
```

### From Source

Prerequisites: Node.js 20+, pnpm 9+

```bash
git clone git@github.com:ruimachado-orbit/lecta.git
cd lecta
make dev
```
